title: veronica repens subsp. repens
intro: Veronica repens subsp. repens, also known as creeping speedwell, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of creeping speedwell include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, creeping speedwell is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
